node.title: Uber | L4 | SDE 2 | Bangalore | India | November 2021 [Offer]

topic.title: Uber | L4 | SDE 2 | Bangalore | India | November 2021 [Offer]

post.content:
**YOE** : 5 years
**Current Organization** : FinTech MNC
**Title** : Senior Software Engineer

I had asked my friend to refer me for L4 role in Uber in September. The process was very slow. It took a month to receive the online coding round invitation. Finally received the invitation in October. 

**Preliminary Round** - Online Coding round
1 Easy Question and few MCQs. Very easy to clear this round.

After nearly a month, I got selected for the Virtual Onsite rounds after few follow-ups. Since I was already preparing for my Google and Facebook interviews, I went straight ahead with the interviews. 

Google Interview Experience - https://leetcode.com/discuss/interview-experience/1658957/Google-or-SDE-2-or-L4-or-Bangalore-or-India-or-November-2021-Offer
Facebook Interview Experience - https://leetcode.com/discuss/interview-experience/1658404/Facebook-or-E4-or-London-or-Virtual-Onsite-or-November-2021-Reject

All rounds are for an hour.

**Virtual Onsite 1** - Coding Round
1 Medium Question - Similar to https://leetcode.com/problems/evaluate-division/ -  You need to construct a graph and do BFS/DFS, there was a follow up, which was to find the path based on priority. Was able to solve this completely without hint.
1 Hard Question - Similar to https://leetcode.com/problems/bus-routes/ -  I came up with a O(N^3). The interviewer gave me a hint, and I quickly figured out the O(N^2) solution.

**Virtual Onsite 2** - Machine Coding Round
Asked to Code an in-memory Job Scheduler which handles 3 types of jobs - Recurring and Non recurring jobs. There can be multiple jobs to be run concurrently. Was tested on code quality, extensibility and handle concurrency.

**Virtual Onsite 3** - System Design Round
Question 1 - Asked to explain the project design from my previous experience. Questions around why I did what I did, compromises in design etc.
Question 2 - Design a system to fetch restaurants delivering in an area, customise the restaurants listings based on user profile.

**Virtual Onsite 4** - Hiring Manager Round / Behavioral Round
Questions about my work and past experience, around leadership principles. Standard HM Behavioral round.

Next day, Recruiter said I got the offer. In the next few days, I got the offer letter. 

**Experience** - I felt the interview process to be structured. Initially there was a delay in getting thru to the interviews. However, the rest of the process happened fast. The interviews, negotiations and offer rollout happened with in 2 weeks. 

You can find my compensation details here - https://leetcode.com/discuss/compensation/1639867/Uber-or-L4-or-Bangalore-or-India-or-Accepted

My preparation journey - https://leetcode.com/discuss/interview-experience/1664001/FAANG-Preparation-Journey

Congrats bro!! one question How did u practice, did u practice Uber tagged questions or u already had solved those type of questions. I\'m asking because the two problems in the first round are quite hard and it\'s difficult to come up with ther algo on the spot. It\'s different if ur a competitive coder
how did you take out from work to prepare? are you coasting on work?
In Virtual Onsite -2, do you need a working code or a buggy code but good SOLID / design patterns and concurrency control would be good enough?
can you share your approach to q2 of onsite 3 - resturant one ?
In onsite 2 were you allowed to use IDE with autocomplete or it was something like a Google doc ?
could you elaborate the followup question in Coding Round Q1?
could you share your approach to  Virtual Onsite 2 ?
could you share the sudo code for Virtual Onsite 2 ?
What are 3 types of jobs in Round 2? Rec and Non-Rec and? Also, what was the duration for each of these rounds
